game_name: Last Battle: Legend of the Final Hero

design_philosophy.core_loop: Linear side-scrolling combat progression with enemy waves, power meter management, and selective level ordering creating strategic routing
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Time pressure (gradual health drain if time expires) balanced against combat encounters; power meter accumulation creates moment-to-moment decision points for transformation timing
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Martial arts master (Aarzak/Kenshiro) progressing through post-apocalyptic world, defeating increasingly powerful opponents with escalating combat abilities
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Hybrid time-limit system that punishes hesitation without instant failure; semi-open level progression within chapters allowing player-chosen routing; transformation mechanic gating superhuman combat states rather than teaching incremental mastery

difficulty.checkpointing: Map-based stage progression with checkpoint between levels; boss encounters and maze stages exempt from time limits
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Power-up meter progression creates scaling difficulty; strategic routing through non-linear level selection within chapters allows difficulty pacing; transformation state availability acts as dynamic difficulty adjustment

progression.upgrade_system: Power meter that fills through combat, enabling temporary super-powered transformation state granting rapid punches/kicks for remainder of stage
progression.unlock_mechanism: Chapter-gated progression with required level completion order within chapters; some levels must be completed in specific sequence to advance
replayability_hooks: Multiple routing options within chapters through non-linear level selection
Ally encounters provide variable stat bonuses encouraging different playthroughs

world_building_techniques: Post-apocalyptic setting inherited from Hokuto no Ken IP (chapters 137-210 of manga)
Character name/style localization changes in Western version obscuring original narrative context
Level progression suggests geographical journey through hostile territory
Boss encounters represent antagonist faction members (Duke/Falco and escaped generals seeking revenge)

boss_design.philosophy: One-on-one martial arts duels punctuating stage progression; distinct encounters against named antagonists from narrative
notable_bosses: Various stage bosses representing faction hierarchy

faction_indicators: Empire structure with military hierarchy (generals, antagonist leadership)
Rebel faction seeking revenge on protagonist
Post-apocalyptic survivor groups providing ally support
danger_signals: Maze stages requiring navigation skill, suggesting environmental hostility
Escalating boss encounters indicating organized opposition
Time pressure mechanics implying urgency/threat from external forces
environmental_storytelling: Four distinct chapters/stages suggesting geographical progression through contested territory
Map interface showing location and available paths implies contested landscape
Dungeon mazes indicate civilizational breakdown and exploration challenges
